facts.legal_description: The land and improvements at the subject premises, being the same parcel conveyed by warranty deed, together with all buildings thereon

docket.entry: Complaint filed against the general contractor and excavation subcontractor.
Answer, cross-claim against the subcontractor, and third-party complaint against the design professional filed.
Scheduling order entered; discovery and expert-disclosure deadlines set.
Order granting destructive testing and site inspection of the foundation.
Expert reports on causation, soils, and repair cost served.
Motion for summary judgment on the mechanic's lien taken under advisement.
Mediation held; discovery continues toward trial.

motions.title: Motion to bifurcate liability and damages
motions.relief: Separate trials on liability and damages.

motions.title: Motion for summary judgment on the mechanic's lien
motions.relief: Judgment reducing or discharging the recorded lien.

motions.title: Motion in limine to exclude a defect expert
motions.relief: Exclusion of the homeowner's causation expert.

motions.title: Motion to consolidate
motions.relief: Consolidation of the related lien and defect actions.

elements: Defective or nonconforming work by the contractor

causes_of_action.claim: Breach of the implied warranty of workmanlike construction
elements: An implied warranty of workmanlike, habitable construction
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Cost to repair and consequential damages

elements: A duty of reasonable care in construction
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Compensatory damages for the cost of repair and diminution in value

elements: A duty to excavate and compact fill to specification
Causation of the settlement and intrusion
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Compensatory damages attributable to the defective sitework

causes_of_action.claim: Enforcement and discharge of the mechanic's lien
elements: A genuine dispute over the amount owed
The homeowner's setoff for defective work
causes_of_action.relief_sought: A declaration reducing or discharging the lien and an accounting

affirmative_defenses.defense: Failure to maintain and improper grading by the owner
affirmative_defenses.basis: The conditions result from the owner's failure to maintain and regrade the site.
The claimed repairs would put the homeowner in a better position than the contract required.
The excavation and fill work passed inspection and complied with the applicable building code.

affirmative_defenses.defense: Lack of notice and opportunity to cure
affirmative_defenses.basis: The homeowner failed to provide notice and a reasonable opportunity to cure the alleged defects.
Claims for design and construction are barred by the period of repose. 14 M.R.S. § 752-A.
